authoritative_sources.title: Monitoring Distributed Systems
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google SRE is a recognized public operations reference for production monitoring, symptom-versus-cause distinction, actionable alerting, dashboards, and human operational response.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: monitoring strategy, signal selection, alert actionability, dashboard versus paging decisions
practical_use_in_pack: symptom versus cause language in incident playbooks

authoritative_sources.title: Alerting on SLOs
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google SRE workbook is a recognized workflow reference for SLO-based alerting, burn-rate, error-budget, and paging-window decisions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: SLO mapping, threshold design, alert-window tradeoff review

authoritative_sources.title: Prometheus Alerting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Prometheus official documentation defines alerting practice and terminology for alert rules, labels, routing, and notification semantics used in common monitoring stacks.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: alert rule review, alert label and route design, alert fatigue reduction

authoritative_sources.title: Programmability Configuration Guide, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x - Model-driven telemetry
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Cisco official IOS XE documentation defines model-driven telemetry roles, collectors, subscribers, subscription types, transports, cadence, prerequisites, and platform limits for network device telemetry.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: vendor telemetry design, collector and subscription review, device telemetry evidence handling
practical_use_in_pack: publisher, receiver, subscriber, and collector terminology

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 2863 - The Interfaces Group MIB
authoritative_sources.publisher: Internet Engineering Task Force / RFC Editor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF RFC 2863 defines interface state and counters used as authoritative terminology for link health, errors, discards, speed, and utilization evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: interface telemetry intake, counter interpretation, packet-loss triage

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 3413 - Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) Applications
authoritative_sources.publisher: Internet Engineering Task Force / RFC Editor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF RFC 3413 defines SNMP application behavior for command generators, notification originators, and notification receivers used in common network event monitoring.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: SNMP polling and trap review, event ingestion validation

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 7854 - BGP Monitoring Protocol (BMP)
authoritative_sources.publisher: Internet Engineering Task Force / RFC Editor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF RFC 7854 defines BGP Monitoring Protocol message and collector behavior for route, peer, and policy visibility in routing monitoring systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: BGP route-churn monitoring, peer-state evidence, route collector review
practical_use_in_pack: route and prefix event evidence requirements

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 8641 - Subscription to YANG Notifications for Datastore Updates
authoritative_sources.publisher: Internet Engineering Task Force / RFC Editor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF RFC 8641 defines subscription behavior for YANG datastore updates used by streaming telemetry collectors and freshness-sensitive network monitoring pipelines.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: streaming telemetry design, subscription freshness, collector evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: Logging IP traffic using VPC Flow Logs
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official VPC documentation defines VPC Flow Logs records, destinations, formats, permissions, and limitations used as cloud network traffic evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: AWS flow telemetry intake, reachability evidence, monitoring coverage review

authoritative_sources.title: CloudWatch metrics for your Application Load Balancer
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS Elastic Load Balancing documentation defines ALB and target-group metric names, dimensions, and health telemetry used in network delivery monitoring.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: load-balancer monitoring, target health review, network versus application boundary analysis

authoritative_sources.title: Monitoring health checks using CloudWatch
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS Route 53 documentation defines health-check monitoring and CloudWatch evidence for DNS failover and reachability checks.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: DNS health monitoring, synthetic check evidence, failover alert review

authoritative_sources.title: What is Azure Network Watcher?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft official documentation defines Azure Network Watcher monitoring, topology, connection monitor, diagnostic tools, flow logs, traffic analytics, and usage limits.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Azure topology and connection monitoring, flow-log review, diagnostic evidence handling

authoritative_sources.title: Connectivity Tests overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google Cloud official documentation defines Network Intelligence Center connectivity-test evidence, configuration analysis, live data-plane analysis, supported configurations, and limitations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Google Cloud reachability evidence, modeled versus live path analysis, provider limitation handling

authoritative_sources.title: SP 800-92 - Guide to Computer Security Log Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-92 provides public guidance for log generation, transmission, storage, analysis, disposal, and log-management policy relevant to telemetry evidence handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: log and flow retention, evidence handling, audit traceability, disposal caveats

authoritative_sources.title: SP 800-53 Rev. 5 - Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 is a recognized control catalog for audit, configuration management, boundary protection, system monitoring, incident response, and contingency evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: control evidence mapping, monitoring recordkeeping, boundary and permission caveat review

authoritative_sources.title: Current committed package baseline for network-monitoring-specialist
authoritative_sources.source_class: Current package task baseline from network-monitoring-specialist
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Repository-owned baseline defines prior supported tasks, common inputs, output shapes, and refusal rules that the refreshed spec preserves where valid and supersedes as source of truth.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: legacy boundary comparison, task preservation, stale assumption replacement
practical_use_in_pack: legacy scenario minimum retained and exceeded
refusal rules refined into spec-first boundaries

workflow_stages.name: monitoring intake and scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Collect request intent, affected path, topology, target services, failure domains, SLO/SLA objectives, current monitoring records, alert policies, owners, retention rules, and authority context before making a monitoring claim.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: signal and evidence mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map DNS, DHCP, routing, BGP, interface, transport reachability, flow-log, synthetic, load-balancer, and alert records to failure domains and source systems.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: threshold, alert, and coverage design
workflow_stages.description: Define SLIs, SLO mappings, metric thresholds, aggregation windows, alert severity, owner routing, dashboards, blind-spot closure, and false-positive or false-negative tradeoffs.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: fault isolation and confidence handling
workflow_stages.description: Use supplied telemetry, incident timeline, topology, and change evidence to rank hypotheses while escalating missing, stale, contradictory, or authority-blocked evidence.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: handoff, refusal, and materialization control
workflow_stages.description: Return to orchestrator or adjacent specialists when work crosses into endpoint, identity, application, forensics, legal, compliance, security operations, incident command, or direct execution, and document safe materialization requirements.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts.name: Escalation memo with required evidence and confidence statements

systems_of_record: IPAM, DNS, DHCP, route-policy, BGP, firewall/NAT, load-balancer, SD-WAN, and cloud-network configuration repositories or approved exports
metrics, logs, SNMP, streaming telemetry, synthetic probe, flow-log, packet-summary, dashboard, and alert-management platforms
cloud provider network telemetry systems such as AWS VPC Flow Logs and CloudWatch, Azure Network Watcher, and Google Cloud Network Intelligence Center
incident, problem, change, post-incident review, runbook, and escalation repositories
tenant privacy, retention, security, contractual availability, change-freeze, and approval records

allowed: monitoring strategy and telemetry coverage recommendations
DNS, DHCP, routing, BGP, interface, transport, flow-log, synthetic, and load-balancer monitoring guidance
SLI/SLO mapping, threshold design, alert severity, owner routing, and noise-control recommendations
runbook, escalation memo, and handoff note production
escalate: required topology, telemetry, SLO, alert-owner, retention, change, or source-system evidence is missing
public source, provider guidance, tenant policy, privacy, retention, contractual, or change-freeze requirements conflict
request needs production execution, approval, incident command, risk acceptance, or legal/compliance authority
tenant-specific facts would materially change the answer but are unavailable
refuse: direct device configuration changes, CLI execution, credentials, cloud console action, or alert-policy deployment
endpoint security policy enforcement or anti-malware tuning
identity federation, directory, or authentication architecture decisions
application performance optimization not directly tied to network telemetry
packet-level forensic lab testing, legal evidence preservation, or capture analysis for legal purposes

domain_failure_modes.why_material: Network monitoring can appear complete while missing critical paths, asymmetric routes, dependency edges, or failure domains.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: thresholds that page on noise but miss real outages
domain_failure_modes.why_material: Connectivity services require actionable alerting with known aggregation windows, SLO impact, and false-positive or missed-detection tradeoffs.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: provider telemetry treated as complete packet truth
domain_failure_modes.why_material: Cloud flow logs, connectivity tests, and load-balancer metrics have provider-specific dimensions, retention, permissions, and visibility limits.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: routing or interface incidents diagnosed without source-of-truth evidence
domain_failure_modes.why_material: BGP churn, interface errors, and asymmetric paths require topology, route, counter, collector, and change evidence before bounded root-cause claims.
Network monitoring evidence can trigger endpoint, identity, application, forensic, or legal questions that this lane must not own.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: tenant policy and public guidance conflict hidden under generic cross-industry language
domain_failure_modes.why_material: Cross-industry monitoring still depends on tenant privacy, retention, contractual availability, and change-control rules.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions
No unreviewed regressions and specification-based materialization readiness

unresolved_ambiguities.description: Public sources can recommend broad telemetry, but tenant privacy, retention, residency, legal hold, and contractual commitments determine what may be collected or stored.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Escalate to tenant privacy, security, legal, or change owners when the facts materially change collection, retention, or recommendation scope.

unresolved_ambiguities.description: Cloud provider network telemetry does not expose every packet-path detail and may differ by resource type, region, feature version, and permissions.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require provider-specific documentation and tenant resource evidence before final coverage claims.
